analyses.name: Fortinet, Inc.
analyses.verdict: The cleanest large-cap tech setup that isn't a semiconductor — a cybersecurity leader breaking out on a tight base with real free cash flow, in the one tech pocket the rotation is rewarding.

financials.summary: Firewall and secure-networking leader with a high-margin, cash-generative model spanning FortiGate appliances, SASE and security operations software. FY2025 delivered mid-teens-plus billings and revenue growth with operating margins in the low-30s% and strong free-cash-flow conversion; the mix continues to shift toward higher-margin software and subscription (FortiGuard security services).
highlights: Operating margin ~low-30s% with heavy FCF conversion

accomplishments: Scaled the unified SASE and security-operations platforms beyond the core firewall franchise
Sustained double-digit product + service billings growth
Maintained category leadership in the secure-networking segment against Palo Alto and Cisco

journey_52w.summary: Trended from a mid-cycle firewall-refresh trough into a strong recovery, ending the year +96% over three months and printing a tight 5-contraction VCP just -2.8% off its high with RSI still only 56 — an un-extended breakout.

milestones.label: H1 FY2025
milestones.note: Firewall refresh cycle re-accelerated billings

milestones.label: H2 FY2025
milestones.note: SASE/SecOps mix lifted margins; stock re-rated higher

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Consolidated into a tight base near highs ahead of Jul 30 earnings

risks: Earnings Jul 30 — a beat is largely expected into the base
A broad tech/growth drawdown would drag it despite leading fundamentals
Firewall demand is somewhat refresh-cycle dependent
catalysts: Continued SASE/SecOps attach driving margin mix
critical.valuation: Premium but not extreme for a cash-generative security leader; TradingView independently rates it Buy.

analyses.name: Assurant, Inc.
analyses.verdict: A specialty insurer at a new-high area on the tightest base on the board — the textbook expression of the financials/insurance rotation.

financials.summary: Specialty insurance and extended-service-contract provider with two segments: Global Housing (lender-placed homeowners) and Global Lifestyle (mobile device protection, vehicle-service contracts). FY2025 saw strong underwriting results and net investment income drive sharp EPS growth, with disciplined capital return via buybacks and dividends.
highlights: Forward P/E ~12 — inexpensive for the growth

accomplishments: Grew the higher-return Global Lifestyle device-protection franchise
Delivered favorable Housing underwriting margins in a benign-catastrophe stretch
Continued double-digit book-value-plus-dividend compounding

journey_52w.summary: Ground steadily higher through the year as insurers led, tightening into a very tight 3-contraction VCP (2.1%) and finishing -1.2% off its high with RSI 67.

milestones.label: FY2025
milestones.note: Underwriting + investment income drove the EPS surge

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Coiled into a 2.1% base near highs ahead of Aug 5 earnings

risks: Catastrophe-loss quarters can dent Housing results
Earnings Aug 5 — event risk
Device-protection tied to smartphone upgrade cycles
critical.valuation: ~12x forward with +91% EPS growth — cheap; TradingView rates it Buy.

analyses.name: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
analyses.verdict: The best-in-class megabank leading a financials rotation, at a new-high area on a clean 5-contraction base with Q2 already reported — no near-term earnings landmine.

financials.summary: The largest and most diversified U.S. bank — consumer, commercial, investment banking, asset & wealth management. FY2025 combined record net interest income with a rebound in markets and investment-banking fees; credit costs remained contained and the fortress balance sheet supported large buybacks and a rising dividend.
highlights: Forward P/E ~14 — reasonable for the quality
Record NII plus recovering capital-markets fees

accomplishments: Gained share across trading and investment banking
Absorbed and integrated prior acquisitions while growing organically
Maintained CET1 strength enabling aggressive capital return

journey_52w.summary: Led the bank complex all year to fresh highs, and after its mid-July Q2 report settled into a tight 5-contraction VCP (2.5%) just -1.0% off its high with RSI 65.

milestones.label: FY2025
milestones.note: Record NII + IB-fee rebound drove EPS +47%

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Q2 reported; stock based tightly near highs (next report ~Oct)

risks: A sharp adverse rate move could pressure NII/the curve trade
Financials-group leadership can rotate if breadth narrows
Credit normalization if the economy softens
critical.valuation: ~14x forward — fair for the franchise; TradingView rates it Buy.

analyses.name: The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc.
analyses.verdict: A constructive -6% pullback into a tight base offers a lower-risk re-entry into the leading investment bank; widely held on this platform.

financials.summary: Premier global investment bank and trading house with a growing asset & wealth management franchise. FY2025 saw a powerful rebound in investment-banking fees and strong trading revenue, driving EPS +92%; the firm continued to lean into fee-based asset management while returning heavy capital via buybacks.
highlights: IB-fee rebound + trading strength drove the surge

accomplishments: Rode the M&A/underwriting recovery to record fee quarters
Grew fee-based asset & wealth management AUM
Executed large share repurchases as capital freed up

journey_52w.summary: Advanced strongly with the capital-markets recovery, then pulled back -6.2% off its high into a 4-contraction VCP (2.4%) with RSI 59 after its mid-July report — a healthier entry than names pinned at highs.

milestones.label: FY2025
milestones.note: IB-fee + trading rebound drove EPS +92%

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Q2 reported; constructive pullback into a tight base (next report ~Oct)

risks: Capital-markets revenue is cyclical — a deal-flow slowdown hits fees
Highest absolute share price here — size accordingly
Trading revenue is inherently volatile
critical.valuation: ~15x forward with a strong ROE recovery; TradingView rates it Buy.

analyses.name: UnitedHealth Group Incorporated
analyses.verdict: The cleanest managed-care recovery — low RSI leaves major room, TradingView independently rates it STRONG BUY, and it sits in the leading healthcare group.

financials.summary: The largest U.S. health insurer and healthcare-services company (UnitedHealthcare + Optum). FY2025 combined membership growth and Optum expansion with a recovery in EPS (+61% off a reset base) as the medical-cost trend stabilized; the company continued to grow Optum Health, Rx and Insight.
highlights: Revenue growth low-single-digit but high absolute scale
Optum services diversification cushioning insurance cyclicality

accomplishments: Grew Optum Health value-based care lives
Stabilized the medical-cost ratio after a difficult prior stretch
Sustained industry-leading scale and cash generation

journey_52w.summary: Recovered from a well-publicized drawdown into a 3-contraction VCP (2.9%), finishing -2.4% off its high with RSI a low 51 — an un-extended recovery with room to run.

milestones.label: FY2025
milestones.note: Medical-cost trend stabilized; EPS recovered +61%

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Based near highs, RSI 51 — plenty of upside room

risks: Elevated regulatory/political and headline risk for managed care
Medical-cost-trend surprises can pressure margins
Large index constituent — macro moves matter
critical.valuation: ~19x forward with a recovering EPS base; TradingView rates it STRONG BUY.

analyses.name: CVS Health Corporation
analyses.verdict: An integrated-healthcare turnaround printing at its high on a tight base, with EPS +63% and the rotation's healthcare-services bid behind it.

financials.summary: Integrated healthcare: retail pharmacy, the Caremark PBM, and Aetna health insurance. FY2025 showed the turnaround taking hold — EPS recovered sharply (+63%) as Aetna's cost trend improved and the pharmacy/PBM franchise stabilized; management continued deleveraging while defending the dividend.
highlights: Aetna cost-trend improvement driving the recovery

accomplishments: Improved Aetna's medical-cost performance versus the prior year
Advanced the integrated care-delivery strategy
Maintained the dividend while paying down debt

journey_52w.summary: Climbed out of a multi-year base into a 4-contraction VCP (2.6%), finishing essentially at its high (-0.1%) with RSI 64.

milestones.label: FY2025
milestones.note: Aetna cost trend improved; EPS recovered +63%

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Tight base at new-high area ahead of Aug 5 earnings

risks: PBM and drug-pricing regulatory scrutiny
Earnings Aug 5 — turnaround must keep proving out
High debt load requires continued deleveraging
critical.valuation: ~13x forward — cheap for a recovering integrated model; TradingView rates it Buy.

analyses.name: Kimco Realty Corporation
analyses.verdict: The REIT leadership expression — a fresh 52-week high with an un-extended RSI, riding the rate-sensitive rotation bid.

financials.summary: The largest U.S. owner of open-air, grocery-anchored shopping centers. FY2025 delivered high occupancy, positive leasing spreads and steady same-property NOI growth; FFO per share advanced and the well-covered dividend rose, backed by a solid investment-grade balance sheet.
highlights: High occupancy with positive leasing spreads

accomplishments: Sustained record or near-record occupancy across the grocery-anchored portfolio
Captured positive re-leasing spreads on expiring space
Integrated prior portfolio additions and recycled capital

journey_52w.summary: Rode the rate-sensitive REIT rotation to a fresh 52-week high while keeping RSI at only 55 — a breakout with room, on a 3-contraction base.

milestones.label: FY2025
milestones.note: Occupancy + leasing spreads lifted FFO

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: New high, RSI 55, ahead of Aug 4 earnings

risks: A back-up in long yields pressures REIT multiples
Earnings Aug 4 — FFO guide matters
Retail tenant credit if consumer softens
critical.valuation: Reasonable FFO multiple with a growing dividend; TradingView rates it Buy.

analyses.name: Howmet Aerospace Inc.
analyses.verdict: The industrials leader — an aerospace-components compounder on a tight 5-contraction base with EPS +71%, riding both the aero cycle and the industrials rotation. Held on the platform.

financials.summary: Maker of engineered aerospace components — engine airfoils, fastening systems, structures and forged wheels. FY2025 rode the commercial-aerospace build-rate recovery and strong aftermarket/spares demand to double-digit revenue growth and sharp margin expansion, driving EPS +71%; the company raised its dividend and bought back stock.
highlights: Margin expansion on aero mix + spares

accomplishments: Captured commercial-aero build-rate recovery and spares demand
Expanded operating margins meaningfully year over year
Grew the high-margin aftermarket business

journey_52w.summary: Trended up with the aerospace cycle all year, tightening into a 5-contraction VCP (2.5%) and finishing -2.2% off its high with RSI 59.

milestones.label: FY2025
milestones.note: Build-rate recovery + spares drove EPS +71%

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Tight base near highs ahead of Aug 6 earnings

risks: Aero supply-chain hiccups or build-rate cuts
Earnings Aug 6 — event risk
Rich-ish valuation on the aero-cycle re-rate
catalysts: OEM build-rate increases + engine spares demand
critical.valuation: Premium multiple justified by the aero upcycle; TradingView rates it Buy.

analyses.name: EOG Resources, Inc.
analyses.verdict: A top-tier low-cost E&P catching a real fundamental tailwind — renewed Middle East tension has oil bid and pump prices back over $4 — on a well-formed 6-contraction base.

financials.summary: One of the largest and lowest-cost U.S. shale producers, known for capital discipline and premium well returns. FY2025 combined production growth with disciplined capex and strong free cash flow; the company returned cash through a base-plus-special dividend and buybacks while holding a net-cash-leaning balance sheet.
highlights: Heavy free-cash-flow return via dividends + buybacks

accomplishments: Grew low-cost production while holding capital discipline
Sustained a differentiated base-plus-special dividend framework
Maintained a fortress, low-leverage balance sheet

journey_52w.summary: Consolidated for much of the year with oil range-bound, then firmed into a 6-contraction VCP (2.9%) at -5.9% off its high with RSI 63 as Middle East escalation lifted crude.

milestones.label: FY2025
milestones.note: Disciplined growth + FCF return; EPS +40%

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Oil bid on Middle East tension; well-formed base ahead of Aug 5 earnings

risks: Oil is the swing factor — a ceasefire could cap the move
Earnings Aug 5
Commodity/E&P volatility
critical.valuation: ~10x forward with heavy FCF return — inexpensive; TradingView rates it Buy.

analyses.name: Monster Beverage Corporation
analyses.verdict: The cleanest staples-growth setup on the board — RSI freshly reset to 49 with real double-digit growth, exactly what a rotation tape rewards.

financials.summary: Energy-drink leader (Monster, Reign, Bang, Java Monster, plus alcohol brands) with a high-margin, asset-light model — Coca-Cola handles distribution. FY2025 saw volume plus pricing drive double-digit revenue growth, gross margins recovering toward the high-50s% as freight and aluminum costs eased, and operating leverage flowing to the bottom line, funding continued buybacks off a net-cash balance sheet.

accomplishments: Pushed further into alcohol/flavored-malt and international markets
Continued gross-margin recovery off the input-cost trough
Sustained energy-category share leadership alongside Red Bull

journey_52w.summary: Based much of the year absorbing input-cost and volume debates, tightened into a 3-contraction VCP (2.6%), and finished -2.6% off its high with RSI a low 49 — among the least-extended leaders.

milestones.label: H2 FY2025
milestones.note: Gross margins recovered as freight and aluminum normalized

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: RSI reset to 49 on a tight base ahead of Aug 7 earnings

risks: Forward P/E ~38 leaves little room for a growth miss
Earnings Aug 7 — event risk
Input-cost or volume-growth deceleration
catalysts: International + alcohol expansion, continued margin recovery
critical.valuation: ~38x forward — premium but supported by double-digit growth and net cash; TradingView rates it Buy.
